title: Energy-Efficient Loop Optimization
description: Demonstrates loop unrolling to reduce loop overhead and save energy
code:
// BEFORE: Standard loop (more iterations, more energy)
FOR i = 0 TO n-1 DO
 result[i] = a[i] * 2
END FOR

// AFTER: Loop unrolling (fewer iterations, less overhead)
FOR i = 0 TO n-1 STEP 4 DO
 result[i] = a[i] * 2
 result[i+1] = a[i+1] * 2
 result[i+2] = a[i+2] * 2
 result[i+3] = a[i+3] * 2
END FOR
explanation: Loop unrolling reduces loop control overhead (increment, comparison, branch) by performing multiple operations per iteration, reducing CPU cycles and energy consumption.

title: Bitwise Multiplication Optimization
description: Using bit shift instead of multiplication for powers of 2
explanation: Bitwise left shift is significantly faster and more energy-efficient than arithmetic multiplication, especially for powers of 2. Modern CPUs handle shifts in single cycles.

title: Lazy Evaluation Example
description: Deferring expensive computations until actually needed
explanation: Lazy evaluation avoids unnecessary computations by deferring calculation until explicitly requested. This saves CPU cycles and energy when not all results are needed.

title: Caching for Energy Efficiency
description: Using cache to avoid redundant expensive operations
explanation: Memoization stores previously computed results to avoid redundant calculations. This dramatically reduces computational complexity from exponential to linear, saving significant energy.

title: Batch Network Requests
description: Grouping multiple network requests to reduce radio activation energy
explanation: Mobile radios consume significant energy when activating. Batching requests reduces the number of radio activations, substantially saving battery power in mobile applications.
